headline: Southwest Airlines' terrible new policy angers customers
summary: Southwest Airlines used to be known for its customer service. In fact, like many people, I preferred to fly Southwest because it didn’t nickel-and-dime like other airlines, and it offered unique options like free checked bags and open seating. headline: Stocks to Watch Thursday: Broadcom, StubHub, FedEx, Trade Desk
summary: ↗️ Broadcom (AVGO): The semiconductor and software maker's quarterly results beat forecasts. Artificial-intelligence revenue more than doubled. Shares rallied roughly 7% in premarket trading. ↗️ FedEx (FDX): The parcel-delivery company said that, where safe, it had resumed its pick-up and drop-off services across the Middle East.

headline: Analyst cuts American to Neutral, favors Delta and United amid fuel cost pressures
summary: Investing.com -- In a note to clients on Thursday, analysts at Rothschild & Co Redburn downgraded American Airlines to Neutral while maintaining a negative stance on Southwest Airlines and reiterating more positive views on Delta Air Lines and United Airlines, citing rising jet fuel prices and growing capacity as key pressures on the U.S. airline sector.

headline: Is Southwest Airlines (LUV) Still Attractive After Recent Share Price Pullback?
summary: If you are wondering whether Southwest Airlines' current share price still offers value, it helps to step back and look at what the market has been pricing in recently. The stock last closed at US$47.15, with a 7 day return of a 5.5% decline and a 30 day return of a 5.0% decline, while the year to date return stands at 14.2% and the 1 year return at 60.9%, compared with 47.9% over 3 years and a 13.4% decline over 5 years. Recent headlines around Southwest often focus on its position in the...
